concerns.text: Task T021 (Method Selection) and T021b (Multicollinearity) are ordered BEFORE T022a (Compositionality Detection). The spec (FR-002) and plan require method selection to be driven by data distribution (zeros/Shapiro-Wilk) and compositionality. T021 explicitly lists 'If compositionality detected (Depends on T022a)' as a condition, but T022a is scheduled after T021. This creates a circular/missing dependency where the decision logic cannot execute without the output of a later task.

Task T013 (marked [X] complete) explicitly depends on consuming the output of T012 ('variable_load_metrics.json'). However, T012 is marked as incomplete ([ ]). A consumer task cannot be marked complete if its producer task is missing. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' ordering rule and breaks the logical flow of the pipeline.

Task T026 (Report Generation) depends on 'timing_evidence.json' (output of T016). T016 is marked as incomplete ([ ]). T026 is also marked as incomplete, but the dependency chain is broken because the prerequisite artifact generation (T016) is not scheduled as a completed prerequisite. The ordering implies T026 can be attempted before T016 is done.

Task T020 (Data Distribution Checks) depends on 'filtered_data.parquet' (output of T014b). T014b is marked as incomplete ([ ]). T020 is marked as incomplete, but the serialization note explicitly states US2 is blocked until T014b completes. The task list does not reflect this strict serialization in the ordering of the incomplete tasks, potentially allowing T020 to be attempted before T014b.

Task T021b (Perfect Multicollinearity detection) is placed in Phase 4 (US2) but the spec (FR-006) and User Story 3 explicitly define collinearity diagnostics (VIF, rank checks) as part of US3. While the task description notes it might be 'if not already caught', placing a US3 requirement task in US2 creates a semantic ordering conflict with the story boundaries defined in the spec.
